item.title: Leverkusen wrestle with inconsistency as brilliant Bayern await
item.description: Unpredictable Bayer Leverkusen host Bundesliga leaders Bayern Munich on Saturday, still battling the inconsistency which threatens to derail their top-four hopes....

item.title: Forest stunned by Midtjylland, Villa beat Lille
item.description: Nottingham Forest suffered a shock 1-0 defeat against Midtjylland in the Europa League last-16 first leg, while Ollie Watkins ended his goal drought as the Aston Villa striker sealed a 1-0 win at Lille on Thursday....

item.title: Australia edge DPRK to reach Women’s Asian Cup semis
item.description: In-form Alanna Kennedy and skipper Sam Kerr produced spectacular strikes as Australia overcame a talented North Korea 2-1 to reach the semi-finals of the Women’s Asian Cup on Friday....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Slot seeks silverware to save Liverpool’s season
item.description: Arne Slot said the ‘bar at Liverpool is always high’ as he targets glory in the FA Cup and Champions League to save the club’s season following a disastrous Premier League title defence....

item.title: Guardiola warns title bid ‘over’ if City lose to West Ham
item.description: Pep Guardiola believes Manchester City’s Premier League title challenge ‘will be over’ if they fail to beat resurgent West Ham on Saturday....

item.title: ‘World’s best’ James signs new six-year Chelsea deal
item.description: Chelsea boss Liam Rosenior hailed Reece James as the ‘best player in world football in his position’ after the captain signed a new six-year deal at Stamford Bridge....

item.title: Election draws spotlight as Barca host Sevilla
item.description: Barcelona welcome Sevilla on Sunday aiming to maintain their La Liga lead on Real Madrid, but the stakes are even higher off the field with the club’s next president to be named that night....News website membership

item.title: Spurs search for salvation, Arsenal ready for title charge
item.description: Troubled Tottenham head to Liverpool on Sunday searching for salvation in their increasingly desperate fight against relegation....

item.title: History-makers Uzbekistan dream big at Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: History-making Uzbekistan are targeting a major upset against South Korea in the Women’s Asian Cup quarter-finals, with coach Kotryna Kulbyte on Friday urging her players to keep dreaming big....

item.title: Iran says no one can exclude it from WC
item.description: Iran says no one can exclude it from the World Cup later this year, in response to President Donald Trump’s warning that their ‘life and safety’ would be at risk in the US....

item.title: MSC, Police FC share spoils in BFL
item.description: Defending champions Mohammedan Sporting Club were held to a 1-1 draw by Police Football Club in their return-leg tie of the Bangladesh Football League at the Shaheed Dhirendranath Datta Stadium in Cumilla on Friday....

item.title: Mehidy takes centre stage in second ODI
item.description: Mehidy Hasan Miraz took the centre stage both with his bowling and enticing an unusual run out in the first innings of the second ODI between Bangladesh and Pakistan at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur on Friday....

item.title: China’s bride trafficking threat
item.description: ON A humid afternoon in a rural village, a young woman receives a message from a man claiming to be a successful Chinese businessman. He tells her she is beautiful. He says he wants to marry her. Within weeks the man appears in person, claiming......

item.title: Does Iran’s new supreme leader matter?
item.description: IRAN’S announcement of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s replacement after his death by US-Israeli bombing came at an unexpectedly rapid pace. Within days, the Assembly of Experts appointed his son, Mojtaba Khamenei, as supreme leader......

item.title: Faith, diversity and the courage to choose
item.description: THE elections are over. The posters are fading from the walls, the slogans have quieted and the heated debates that filled tea stalls and living rooms across the country are slowly dissolving into reflection....

item.title: Education expansion without economic absorption
item.description: THE promise of higher education has long been linked to the promise of economic progress. Countries that lead the global economy have also invested heavily in strong and responsive systems of higher education. Universities are expected to......

item.title: Why stability must come first
item.description: THE economic debate is increasingly about a familiar policy question: should the country alter its monetary policy to accelerate growth? People are calling for lower interest rates and a stronger push toward growth since inflation is still hurting......

item.title: Corruption watchdog must begin work soon
item.description: THE Anti-Corruption Commission has been running without its chair and commissioners for more than a week, leaving the principal agency that fights against corruption in a state of operational paralysis. The leadership vacuum emerged on March 3......

item.title: An embarrassing presidency
item.description: THE change in the tone, content and underlying allegiance in the speeches of the president, particularly in the wake of the shifts inpoliticalpower, brings into focus the constitutional paradoxes that define the office of the presidency. Recent speeches......

item.title: 43 arrested in special drive in Ctg, 2.5 lakh Yaba seized
item.description: The Chattogram Metropolitan Police has conducted a special drive in various police stations in the city, arresting 43 individuals and recovering 2.5 lakh contraband Yaba pills....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Religious leaders demand end to attack on Iran
item.description: Leaders of Al-Quds Committee Bangladesh on Friday at a human chain formed in Dhaka called on the United States and Israeli governments to stop their military attacks on Iran immediately....

item.title: Chhatra Shakti DU leader’s house set on fire in M’singh
item.description: Unidentified miscreants early Friday set fire to a room of the village home of Jatiya Chhatra Shakti leader Md Saifullah at Dhobaura upazila of Mymensingh....

item.title: Australia contributes A$16.5m in Rohingya support
item.description: The Government of Australia and the United Nations Population Fund have reaffirmed their partnership to support Rohingya refugees and host communities in Bangladesh through a new A$16.5 million multi-year funding agreement for 2026–2028....

item.title: Russian missile kills three in east Ukraine
item.description: A Russian strike in eastern Ukraine on Friday killed three people on a bus near the embattled town of Kupiansk, which Moscow’s army is battling to recapture, investigators said....News website membership

item.title: 2 killed as timber-laden truck plunges into gorge
item.description: Two people were killed when a timber-laden truck fell into a deep gorge at Lama in Bandarban on Thursday night....

item.title: New Nepal govt faces old challenges
item.description: Nepal’s rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah is set to become the next prime minister after his centrist Rastriya Swatantra Party won a commanding majority in parliamentary elections....

item.title: Businessman detained for hoarding diesel in Magura
item.description: The police detained a businessman on charges of illegally stockpiling a large quantity of diesel at Mohammadpur in Magura on Friday morning....

item.title: Dharla regaining life as dredging goes on
item.description: Once known for relentless riverbank erosion and declining navigability, the Dharla River in Kurigram is slowly regaining its vitality as an ongoing dredging project deepens the riverbed and stabilises its flow....Bangladesh news updates

item.title: EKUSHEY BOOK FAIR: Readers want quality children’s books
item.description: A huge number of children’s books—including comics, rhymes, fairy tales, and short stories—are published during the book fair, which requires coordination from the production team to maintain quality....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Newly-wed couple, 12 other Rampal road accident victims buried
item.description: All 14 people, including a newly-wed couple and their 11 relatives, killed in Thursday’s head-on collision between a bus and a microbus in the Belaibridge area of Rampal upazila in Bagerhat district on the Khulna-Mongla highway, were laid to rest in......

item.title: Cancellation of trade deal with US demanded
item.description: Economist Anu Muhammad on Friday said that the interim government should be tried as people’s enemy for signing the Agreement on Reciprocal Trade with the United States on February 9 ignoring Bangladesh’s interest....

item.title: Irregular rainfall cuts 2025 tea output in major hubs
item.description: Irregular rainfall affected tea production in major tea-growing regions of Sylhet and Chattogram in 2025, although the country’s overall output slightly increased compared with the previous year....

item.title: 250 groups oppose additional spending on Trump’s illegal war on Iran
item.description: Members of Congress should vote against any additional funding for president Donald Trump’s unconstitutional war on Iran, more than 200 groups said Friday in a letter sent to Congress, reports American Civil Liberties Union....News website membership

item.title: D-8 summit postponed
item.description: An April summit of the D-8 bloc of Muslim-majority developed countries that includes Iran has been postponed due to the war in the Middle East, host Indonesia said Friday....

item.title: Blast rocks Iran, leaders rally in show of defiance
item.description: Deadly explosions rocked Tehran close to a pro-government rally attended by top officials Friday, as Israel and Iran unleashed fresh strikes in a war that has ignited the Middle East and threatens to torpedo the world economy....

item.title: Maldives gas explosion: 5 Bangladeshis killed, 2 injured
item.description: At least five Bangladeshi nationals were killed and two others injured in a gas explosion early Friday at Dhiguraa in the Maldives....

item.title: Slogans in parliament ‘inappropriate’: speaker
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad speaker retired major Hafiz Uddin Ahmed said on Friday that while opposition lawmakers had the democratic right to stage a walkout, chanting slogans inside the parliamentary chamber was ‘inappropriate’ and not in line with established......Bangladesh news updates

item.title: Condolence motions on war crimes convicts decried
item.description: Differentpoliticaland cultural organisations on Friday protested against the 13th Jatiya Sangsad’s adoption of the condolence motion on war crimes convicts related to the country’s 1971 war of independence....

item.title: Non-performing recruiting agencies deepen crisis
item.description: Recruiting agencies in Bangladesh have largely failed to fulfil their core responsibilities of exploring new overseas labour markets and producing skilled manpower, while government authorities have continued issuing licences without ensuring......

item.title: Nine more Bangladeshis injured in ME war
item.description: Nine more Bangladeshi nationals have been injured in the continuing conflict in the Middle East, raising the number of injured migrants to 23, while at least four Bangladeshis have been killed since the war began on February 28....

item.title: Long, complex procedure for curriculum approval decried
item.description: The procedures for revising and approving pre-university and university curriculums in the country’s education system are lengthy and complex, affecting the quality of education, observed educationists and researchers....

item.title: Practical Action holds awareness progs on plastic waste use
item.description: The Practical Action in collaboration with the Narayanganj City Corporation has undertaken several programmes to increase awareness on plastic waste use....
